content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886
May 24, 2006

Louis R. Calcagno, 2nd District
Monterey County, Board of Supervisors
P.O. Box 787
Castroville, CA 95012

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-088

Dear Mr. Calcagno:

This letter confirms the receipt of your May 11th, May 12th and May 17, 2006, request for advice. We must decline your request for advice since that would amount to advice on past conduct. Telephone advice, as informal assistance, does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice (section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3) copy enclosed).

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. In addition, your current question involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111.

Therefore, your request is withdrawn.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division
